title: Conjecture 1.1 (Lin et al.)
statement_text: For $t \geq 88$, a $t$-minimal tree has $t + \lfloor t/11 \rfloor - 1$ vertices.
context_text: Lin et al. determined $t$-minimal trees (trees with $t$ leaves and minimum ABC-index) for all $t \leq 219$ via computer-aided calculations and observed a structural pattern. Based on these computations they proposed this conjecture about the vertex count of $t$-minimal trees for all $t \geq 88$. The present paper shows that the pattern eventually changes as $t$ grows.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture by proving that for $t \geq 1195$ the unique $t$-minimal tree $T_t$ has $t + \lfloor t/10 \rfloor + 1$ or $t + \lfloor t/10 \rfloor + 2$ vertices (depending on $t \bmod 10$), not $t + \lfloor t/11 \rfloor - 1$ as conjectured; the correct structure and exact ABC-index are fully characterised.
notes: PDF source; the floor-bracket characters appear as (cid:98)/(cid:99) in the raw extraction but the mathematical statement is unambiguous from context.
paper_title: The structure of ABC-minimal trees with given number of leaves
